---
title: "RONG WEN"
---

::: {data-aos="fade-up"}
# 🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: RONG WEN
**Faculty:** [ICDI]
*(Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)*
:::

---

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%" data-aos="fade-right"}
### 📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)
![](images/clipboard-1361200114.png){.rounded}
:::

::: {.column width="4%" }
:::

::: {.column width="48%" data-aos="fade-left"}
### 🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)
::: {.ratio .ratio-16x9}
According to the assignment, our vlog focuses on highlighting favorite zones and recommended photo spots for students.

::: {.ratio .ratio-16x9 style="border-radius: 20px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(61, 120, 209, 0.5); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1);"}
<iframe
  src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/MZ1pWb1icTU"
  title="NARIT Shorts Vlog"
  frameborder="0"
  allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share"
  allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::
:::
:::
:::

---

::: {data-aos="fade-up"}
### 💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)
The visit made astronomy feel real and practical. The exhibits helped me connect ideas like light and observation to what scientists actually measure. I liked the balance between excitement and scientific method: tools, careful checks, and clear explanations. Overall, it motivated me to learn the
sky with a more data-driven mindset.

### 💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)
Observation means recording measurable
data (light, time, position).
Instruments and settings change what
details we can detect.
Asking 'how do we know?' improves
scientific thinking

### 🚀 Future Applications (1pt)
I will use a sky map app to identify
constellations and planets and keep a
simple weekly “sky log” (date, location, what
I saw)
:::
